shareholder_structure_note: National and Foreign Corporation controlling shareholder (not further detailed in filing).

key_red_flags: Severely distressed financials: FY2020 revenue collapsed to Rp3.72 bn (vs Rp19.18 bn in 2019); net loss Rp383.2 bn (vs Rp68.3 bn in 2019) driven by Rp370.4 bn 'other expenses' tied to subsidiary disposal; total assets fell 82% YoY to Rp471.7 bn; equity dropped from Rp741 bn to Rp84 bn; non-controlling interests turned negative (-Rp109.3 bn); auditor issued QUALIFIED opinion; audit report dated 13 Nov 2022 (~22 months after year-end, indicative of ongoing issues); gross loss of Rp4.84 bn (COGS > revenue); large subsidiary disposal (Rp370.4 bn, likely non-cash settlement/asset write-down); filing contains no operational disclosures (no planted area, FFB, CPO data). Company appears to be in wind-down or restructuring.
overall_comment: PT Golden Plantation Tbk is a small Indonesian oil palm plantation holding company (IDX: GOLL) with plantation subsidiaries in South Sumatra and Jambi. The 2020 financial statements filing (submitted Nov 2022) is extremely limited - only an audited financial statements package without narrative annual report content. Financials show a company in severe distress: revenue only Rp3.72 bn, net loss Rp383.2 bn, equity nearly wiped out, and a qualified audit opinion. Major subsidiary disposal in 2020 reduced total assets by 82% YoY. No operational metrics (planted area, FFB/CPO production, mill capacity) are disclosed in the filing.

key_red_flags: NOT A PALM OIL COMPANY - processes coconut (kelapa) not oil palm (kelapa sawit). Very small scale (revenue only Rp35.7 bn). overall_comment: PT Indo Pureco Pratama Tbk is a small-cap COCONUT (not palm) oil processor based in Subang, West Java. Main products: Crude Coconut Oil (86.08% of sales) and Copra Meal/Bungkil Kelapa (13.92%). Total assets Rp301.27 bn largely from fixed assets Rp201.3 bn (factory) and advances Rp86.4 bn. Added Rp7 bn long-term bank loan in 2023. Listed on IDX since Dec 2021. Should likely be excluded from palm oil long-list - this is a coconut processor, not palm plantation/refiner.
business_model: Downstream (coconut processing - buys copra/coconut raw materials from third-party suppliers)

Dividend of Rp121/share paid from 2023 profits.

key_red_flags: Small cap, high gearing (Liab/Equity 141.28%, Total Liab/Assets 58.55%); net profit margin declined to 5.19% (2023: 7.23%); only ~1,808 ha of planted area reserves; 100% domestic sales; Debt of Rp798.7 bn bank loans. Plantation area only 7,424 Ha HGU; heavy reliance on third-party FFB (68% of processed FFB).
overall_comment: Small-cap Indonesian palm oil operator in Central Kalimantan. 2024 revenue +41.6% to Rp1,007 bn driven by CPO segment (+37.8%) and new PKS KSO subsidiary acquired end-2023. Operating profit +69.7% to Rp106.4 bn but net profit only +1.7% to Rp52.2 bn due to higher finance/other costs. Total assets Rp1,518 bn, Equity Rp629 bn. CPO production 69,967 t (+21%), PK production 13,367 t (+33.8%). New PKO production of 3,531 t. CFO Rp114 bn (+590% yoy). Dividend paid Rp12 bn (Rp1/share).
business_model: Integrated (upstream plantation + CPO/PK/PKO mills + organic fertilizer via MHL)

core_region: South Sumatra / West Kalimantan / Central Kalimantan
group_structure_note: Controlled by PT Golden Zaga Indonesia (28.37%) and Tjandra Mindharta Gozali (26.37%) as President Commissioner; operates plantations through subsidiaries. New subsidiary SKM (PT Sinar Karya Mandiri, W. Kalimantan) consolidated from Feb 2024.
subsidiaries_note: Suryabumi (99.50%, Sumsel), Cahya Vidi (99.50%, Sumsel), Bumimas (99.50%, investment), Telaga Sari (99.99%, Kalteng), Palma (99.99%, investment), Pemdas (71.07%, Sumsel), SKM (99.99%, Kalbar, acquired Feb 2024 via Suryabumi), plus non-operational Siberut, Mentawai, Mitra Lintas, Siberut GPP, Mentawai GPP.
shareholder_structure_note: PT Golden Zaga Indonesia 28.37%, Tjandra Mindharta Gozali 26.37%, Nyamdorj Chuluunbaatar 9.85%, Jamal Rosyidin Hakki 0.30%, Andrew Michael Vincent 0.00%, Public <5% 35.11%. 14,817 total shareholders (local 85.5% / foreign 14.5%).
primary_business: Oil palm plantation (upstream only) - FFB harvest, CPO and palm kernel processing through subsidiaries; small-scale producer (~0.07% of national planted area).
key_red_flags: Small scale operator (<0.07% national area); FFB processed down 9% YoY (227k->207k t); CPO production down 13% YoY; OER declined to 21.25% (from 22.16%) due to El Nino drought; only 1 mill at Suryabumi (90 T/hr) - Kalimantan estates (TSP, SKM) sell FFB since they have no processing facility; gearing rose to 22% (from 14%); liquidity ratio fell to 0.56x (current ratio below 1); net working capital negative Rp(224.6) bn.
overall_comment: Small pure-upstream palm producer in Sumsel/Kalimantan. 2024 revenue +8% to Rp804.6 bn on higher CPO prices (+18% to Rp13.12m/t) offsetting 25% CPO volume decline. Net profit jumped 26x to Rp62.4 bn (from Rp2.4 bn) on improved gross margin (20% vs 13%). SKM acquisition added 9,610 ha landbank (total 26,798 ha). Planted area grew to 15,596 ha (89% mature). Old ageing profile: 66% of mature trees >10 yrs, avg 16 yrs. Capex only Rp22 bn vs plan Rp93 bn - cash prioritized for working capital. ISPO certification extended to SKM.

hq: Kawasan Industri BIIE Hyundai, Cikarang Selatan, Bekasi, Jawa Barat, Indonesia (Head Office)
core_region: Java (Cikarang, West Java + Pati, Central Java)
group_structure_note: Family-controlled holding with 4 individual founders/family members holding 80% (Tjokro Gunawan 24%, Ivan Cokro Saputra 24%, Cecilia Lanny Budiman 16%, Bernice Cokrosaputro 16%). Operates 3 plants: two in Cikarang (West Java) and one in Pati (Central Java).
subsidiaries_note: PT Cubic Indonesia (89%, industrial metal works/coating services), PT Mitrapak Eramandiri (32.5%, associate, soap/household cleaning/cosmetics), PT Ciptapangan Sukses Makmur (80%, food & beverage wholesale trade), PT Gunanusa Agro Internasional Plantation (99%, in liquidation), Gunanusa Eramandiri International Sdn. Bhd. (100%, liquidated Jan 2025), PT Farmer Gunanusa Eramandiri (40% associate, in liquidation)
shareholder_structure_note: Tjokro Gunawan 24%, Ivan Cokro Saputra 24%, Cecilia Lanny Budiman 16%, Bernice Cokrosaputro 16%, Public 20% (post-IPO)
primary_business: Nut processing & food manufacturing (NOT palm oil). Core products: chopped/sliced/whole almonds, cashews, hazelnuts, pistachios, walnuts, peanuts; nut milks; peanut butter/paste; snack nuts under brands Almonesia, John Farmers, Kagil, GENuts 2 Naga. Contract manufacturing/toll services (55.48% of revenue) is largest segment; key customer PT Unilever Indonesia ~56% of revenue.
key_red_flags: NOT A PALM OIL COMPANY - GUNA is a nut processing and consumer goods (food) manufacturer. Customer concentration risk: PT Unilever Indonesia ~56% of revenue, which suffered boycott-driven -7% sales decline in 2024. Operating cash flow turned negative (-Rp8.34 bn in 2024 vs +Rp125.08 bn in 2023). Short-term bank debt jumped 319% to Rp65 bn (HSBC import credit). Net profit -17% YoY. Two subsidiaries (GAI, GEI Sdn Bhd) being liquidated. Included in palm peer list appears to be erroneous; KBLI 10411 (crude vegetable oil) is a listed business scope but NOT active (explicitly excludes palm oil). Only linkage is a dormant subsidiary named 'Gunanusa Agro International Plantation' (0 assets, being liquidated).
overall_comment: PT Gunanusa Eramandiri Tbk is a family-controlled Indonesian nut processor and food manufacturer that IPO'd on 9 July 2024 (raised Rp75 bn, listed 20% of shares). 2024 revenue grew 3.1% to Rp1,444 bn (US$~90 mn) but net profit fell 17% to Rp78.6 bn due to rising raw material costs and wage inflation. Core segments: contract manufacturing/toll services (55.5%, serving Unilever, Campina, Indofood CBP, Mayora, Arnott's etc.), own-brand nut product sales (39.6%), others (4.9%). Three processing plants (2 Cikarang, 1 Pati) produce ~9,200 tons of nuts/year (actual 2024: 8,314 tons). Total assets Rp980 bn (+29%), equity Rp679 bn (+24%). IMPORTANT: this is NOT a palm oil company - inclusion in the Indonesian palm peer list appears incorrect or is aspirational (one dormant plantation subsidiary is being liquidated).
business_model: Other (Nut processing/food manufacturer, B2B contract manufacturing + own-branded nut products)

group_structure_note: Parent PT FKS Multi Agro Tbk; ultimate parent FKS International Investments Pte. Ltd. (Singapore). Direct subsidiaries: PT Nusa Prima Logistik (65%), PT FKS Solusi Logistik (99.99%), PT FKS Pangan Nusantara (99.99%).
subsidiaries_note: PT Nusa Prima Logistik (Jakarta, 65%, warehousing/logistics), PT FKS Solusi Logistik (Jakarta, 99.99%, consulting & management services), PT FKS Pangan Nusantara (Jakarta, 99.99%, trading food products)
shareholder_structure_note: PT FKS Corporindo Indonesia 79.06%; PT Caturkartika Perdana 10.42%; Public 10.52% (480,000,000 shares total)
primary_business: Distribution and trading of agricultural commodities (feed ingredients, food ingredients) with supporting warehousing, port handling, transportation and logistics.
key_red_flags: Reporting currency is USD (not IDR); revenue declined 7.7% YoY; net profit collapsed 61.2% YoY due to commodity price volatility and margin compression (gross margin 4.1% vs 5.1% prior year); high dependence on imported feed/food commodities; USD/IDR depreciation impact; equity declined YoY; opened new cargo-handling capacity at SGT Cilegon (2x1,000 tph).
overall_comment: Agri-commodity trading & distribution company (feed/food ingredients, logistics); NOT a palm-oil producer. 2024 revenue USD1,292 mn (-7.7% YoY); net profit slumped to USD9.6 mn (-61.2%), with ROA falling to 1.8% (2023: 5.0%). Share price rallied sharply in H2-2024 (Dec close Rp10,650 vs Rp5,525 in 2023, +93%). Limited relevance for palm upstream peer benchmarking except as trading/logistics reference. No plantation/mill/FFB data applicable.

hq: Jakarta, Indonesia (plantations in North & South Sumatra)
group_structure_note: CSRA holds 6 subsidiaries: PT Samukti Karya Lestari (SKL), PT Sukses Sawit Gasing (SSG), PT Abiputra Bina Inter (ABI), PT Ina Zefanya Ataya (IZA), PT Daya Agro Lestari (DAL) 99.99%; PT Bintang Kenten Lestari (BKL) 99.00%. Two mills operational; third mill under construction in Banyuasin, South Sumatra (mid-2025 target).
subsidiaries_note: PT SKL (Tapanuli Selatan - 45 tph PKS 2); PT SSG, PT ABI (Banyuasin, South Sumatra); PT IZA (Musi Rawas Utara); PT DAL (Musi Rawas); PT BKL (Banyuasin - 3rd PKS under construction)
shareholder_structure_note: PT Sapta Sawit Lestari 38.00%; PT Verdan Sawit Lestari 38.00%; PT Sawit Inti Perkasa 4.00%; Public 20.00%. Ultimate: Gita Sapta Adi family (Seman Sendjaja, Alfatiana Avianti, Johanna Wirjoprawiro etc.)
primary_business: Upstream oil palm plantation and CPO milling (2 operating mills, 1 under construction)
key_red_flags: FFB production 319,085 MT only 79.51% of target; yield dropped to 18.2 t/ha (from 19.4); FFB processed increased 16% but FFB own production -5%; increasing reliance on 3rd party FFB (33,723 MT, up 28%); long-term liabilities +30% YoY on new bank loans for 3rd mill construction.
overall_comment: CSRA rebounded in 2024: revenue +22% to Rp1,067bn driven by higher CPO prices (Rp12,793/kg vs Rp10,821); net profit +47% to Rp215bn; EBITDA Rp442bn (margin 41%). Two mills operational at 45 tph each (90 tph total). Third mill at PT BKL Banyuasin (South Sumatra) in construction, target Jun-2025. First dividend since 2022: Rp30bn or Rp14.63/share paid June 2024 (from 2023 profits). Capex Rp228bn mainly for 3rd mill.

group_structure_note: Holding company of Mahkota Group (established 2011). Controlled by PT Mahkota Global Investama (56.40%) and MGI International Holdings Ltd. (22.79%); ultimate beneficial owner is Mily. Family-linked shareholders: Mily, Lily, Usman Sarsi (22.5% each in MGI), Edhie Suwidar (6.5%), Usli & Fuad Halimoen (10% each), Nani (6%).
subsidiaries_note: Direct subsidiaries: PT Mutiara Unggul Lestari (MUL, 92.01%, palm oil processing, Riau); PT Berlian Inti Mekar (BIM, 99.9%, palm oil processing, Riau/South Sumatra); PT Dumai Paricipta Abadi (DPA, 71.5%, storage tanks, Dumai-Riau). Entities from subsidiaries: PT Intan Sejati Andalan (ISA, 99.2%, processing/refinery/fractionation/KCP, Riau); PT Karya Pratama Niagajaya (KPNJ, 52%, palm oil processing, North Sumatra); PT Mega Sawit Sriwijaya (MSS, 52%, palm oil plantation, South Sumatra). Associate: PT Karya Mitra Andalan (KMA, 40%, not active, North Sumatra).
shareholder_structure_note: PT Mahkota Global Investama 56.40%, MGI International Holdings Ltd. 22.79%, Public (<5% each) 19.99%, Treasury 0.82%
primary_business: Integrated palm oil processing (CPO, PK, RBDPO, PFAD, Olein, Stearin, PKO, PKE) + refinery + storage tank leasing + upstream palm plantation (early-stage)
key_red_flags: Net loss 3rd consecutive year (Rp22.98 bn in 2024 vs Rp172.27 bn in 2023, Rp34.79 bn in 2022); DER jumped to 508.80% (2023: 414.37%); current ratio only 87.03% (<100%); heavy reliance on third-party FFB (plantation segment only 486,707 t FFB, own planted area 477.85 ha); bad-debt write-offs at plantation segment caused large losses in 2024; gross margin recovered to 7.52% but still thin.
overall_comment: Sumatra-focused integrated palm processor/refiner (no significant upstream yet). 2024: revenue Rp4.69 tn (-13.6% YoY) on lower sales volumes across CPO/PK/RBDPO/PFAD; COGS fell 16% keeping gross margin at 7.5%. Despite operating profit recovery (Rp68.7 bn vs -Rp43.9 bn loss 2023), high finance costs (Rp100.9 bn) drove net loss of Rp22.98 bn. Refinery 2nd line commissioned in 2024 at PT Intan Sejati Andalan; refinery capacity now 2x1,800 t/day. Biogas and fertilizer plants completed. Stronger balance sheet leverage (Total liabilities Rp2.57 tn, Equity Rp505 bn).

hq: Batulicin, Tanah Bumbu, South Kalimantan, Indonesia
group_structure_note: Part of Jhonlin Group. Controlling shareholder PT Eshan Agro Sentosa (EAS) 86.64%; ultimate controlling family H. Samsudin Andi Arsyad / H. Sudirman Andi Arsyad / Jhony Saputra via PT Jhonlin Group.
subsidiaries_note: None (standalone). JAL merged into JARR in Nov 2023 (entities under common control). Plasma partner: Koperasi Jasa Maju Mulia Sejahtera (518.12 ha).
shareholder_structure_note: PT Eshan Agro Sentosa 86.64% (Controller), PT Sinar Bintang Mulia 0.07%, PT Jhonlin Agro Mandiri 0.05%, Public 13.25% (total 9,230,665,050 shares as of 31 Dec 2024)
primary_business: Biodiesel (FAME) production + integrated palm oil plantation + CPO/cooking oil refinery. FAME remains primary revenue driver via Pertamina/AKR/Andifa contracts.
key_red_flags: Revenue -13% YoY (lower FAME + FFB sales); FAME volume fell to 272,596 KL (down from 310,678 KL in 2023); FFB production only 65% of 244,080 t target (actual 159,688 t); still highly leveraged (D/E 141%, gross debt Rp1.94 tn); customer concentration on biodiesel buyers (Pertamina Patra Niaga, AKR Corporindo, Andifa Perkasa Energi); auditor change to Heliantono & Rekan (from Kanaka Puradiredja Suhartono).
overall_comment: 2024 delivered strongly improved profitability despite lower revenue. Revenue Rp3,865 bn (-13% YoY) but net profit surged to Rp260.7 bn (+237% YoY) on sharp GP margin recovery to 12.5% (vs 8.7%) as own-PKS CPO internally sourced ~15% of needs, cutting third-party CPO cost. Total planted area 18,226 ha (10,668 ha mature); HGU 27,937 ha. Auditor changed to Heliantono & Rekan. EBITDA/sales ratio 6.15 reported; D/E 141%; FAME sales 272,596 KL. Plans to lift internal CPO to 20-25% in 2025.

core_region: Java (rubber, tea, coffee) + Kalimantan Selatan (oil palm + rubber)
group_structure_note: Controlled by PT Sarana Agro Investama (SAI) 92.499%; SAI ultimate beneficial owners: Irwan A. Dinata 26.58%, Djaja Mulia 21.3%, Adi I. Hartono 24.37%, T Marto Sutiono 19.09%, Mohammad Syahrial 6.45%, Rachmat Saptaman 2.21%; PT GMT Kapital Asia 99.96%. Acquired by SAI from PT Sinar Kasih Abadi (SKA) in Sept 2017.
subsidiaries_note: 12 operating plantation subsidiaries: PT Agri Bumi Sentosa (ABS 99%, palm+rubber, S.Kalimantan), PT Kintap Jaya Wattindo (KJW 99.37%, palm+rubber, S.Kalimantan), PT Anugrah Wattiendo (AW 99.68%, palm, S.Kalimantan), PT Indo Java Rubber Planting Co (IJR 99.90%, rubber, Cilacap), PT Kaliduren Estate (KE 99%, tea+rubber+coffee, Jember/Sukabumi), PT Perkebunan Kroewoek (PK 99%, rubber, Bayah), PT Bumi Prada (BP 99.98%, rubber, S.Kalimantan), PT Mulyaningsih (MN 99.01%, coffee+rubber, Jember), PT Banjoemas Landen (BL 99%, rubber, Cilacap), PT Corah Mas Keputren Estate (CMK 99.01%, tea+rubber+coffee, Jember), PT Perkebunan Biting (PB 99%, rubber, Kendal), PT Cipanyusuhan (CP 99%, rubber, Lebak); plus PT Toraja Agra Wattie (Sulawesi).
shareholder_structure_note: PT Sarana Agro Investama 92.50% (15,015,345,027 shares), Rohadi (President Commissioner) 0.001%, Public 7.499% (1,217,474,315 shares). Total outstanding: 16,232,951,842 shares. April 2024 share adjustment to comply with OJK free float rule.
listed_status: Listed (IDX: JAWA) since 20 May 2011; private placement (PMTHMETD) Nov 2023 increased shares to 16.23 bn; moved from Main Board to Development Board on 24 May 2024; suspended 31 July-6 Aug 2024 for late LK filing.
primary_business: Diversified plantation: rubber (dominant by area), palm oil (2 mills), tea, coffee - integrated upstream plantation with downstream processing (POM, rubber sheet/crumb plants, tea/coffee factories)
key_red_flags: Net loss 4th consecutive year (Rp194 bn); equity ratio 35% (improved from 27% after 2023 rights issue); bank debt Rp995 bn + related-party (SAI) zero-interest loans Rp1.16 tn (2024 new Rp1.25 tn approved); temporary trading suspension July 2024 due to late LK filing + unpaid fines; moved to Development Board; CFO persistently negative (-Rp179 bn); goodwill fully written off in 2024; tree productivity mixed (CPO extraction rate fell from 20.68% to 19.82%).
overall_comment: Small diversified Indonesian plantation group (rubber + palm oil + tea + coffee) controlled by PT Sarana Agro Investama (92.5%). 2024 revenue +19.5% to Rp1,071 bn on higher rubber/tea/coffee sales and prices, gross profit swung to positive Rp110 bn (2023: -Rp16 bn); net loss narrowed 36% to Rp194 bn. Equity nearly doubled vs 2022 via shareholder loans converted to equity support. Total planted 43,987 ha (palm 37,475 + plasma 6,512 ha nucleus combined; rubber ~15,130 ha across subsidiaries). 2 POMs (Kintap + ABS Marabahan). Moved to Development Board during trading suspension episode in 2024.

company: PT Perusahaan Perkebunan London Sumatra Indonesia Tbk
group_structure_note: Part of Salim Group / Indofood Group. Direct parent: PT Salim Ivomas Pratama Tbk (SIMP) 59.51%; indirect via Indofood Agri Resources 0.11%. Ultimate parent: First Pacific Company Limited (HK-listed), controlled by Anthoni Salim.
subsidiaries_note: Direct: PT Multi Agro Kencana Prima (MAKP, 99.99%), Lonsum Singapore Pte. Ltd. (LSP, 100%), PT Tani Musi Persada (TMP, 99.92%), PT Sumatra Agri Sejahtera (SAS, 99.99%), PT Tani Andalas Sejahtera (TAS, 99.92%), Agri Investments Pte. Ltd. (AIPL, 100%), PT Wushan Hijau Lestari (WHL, 65%). Indirect: Sumatra Bioscience Pte. Ltd., PT Perusahaan Perkebunan, Perindustrian dan Perdagangan Umum Pasir Luhur (PL, 64.98%).
shareholder_structure_note: PT Salim Ivomas Pratama Tbk 59.51%; Indofood Agri Resources Ltd. 0.11%; Director Ferdi Gunawan 0.003%; Public (<5% each) 40.38%. Total 6,819,963,965 shares.
primary_business: Integrated upstream plantation: oil palm (91,152 ha nucleus), rubber (16,231 ha), cocoa (2,822 ha), tea (866 ha); oil palm seeds via SumBio R&D
key_red_flags: ~80% of sales to parent SIMP (related-party concentration); ageing oil palm trees with slight declining production trend (FFB nucleus 1.173mt in 2024 vs 1.295mt in 2020); no bank debt (net cash position) but heavy parent dependency.
overall_comment: Established 1906 in Medan; Indonesia's oldest palm plantation company. 2024 revenue +9% to Rp4.56tn driven by CPO price rebound (+17% domestic KPB to Rp13,190/kg), partly offset by 8% CPO sales volume decline. Net profit +94% to Rp1.48tn. EBITDA +61% to Rp2.10tn (46% margin). Debt-free with Rp5.45tn cash. 97% of nucleus CPO is ISPO-certified. Oil palm 94% of sales, rubber 3%, seeds 1%, others 2%.

core_region: Java/Surabaya (HQ); candy factory in Bogor West Java (leased out); coffee-bean trading operations national
group_structure_note: Small trading-industrial company. No disclosed subsidiaries; standalone (non-consolidated) financials. Controlling shareholders PT Hijau Sari (36.07%) and PT Mitra Niaga Sakti (32.44%).
subsidiaries_note: None disclosed in the AR (standalone financial statements).
shareholder_structure_note: PT Hijau Sari 36.07%, PT Mitra Niaga Sakti 32.44%, PT Surya Pelangi Mandiri 8.06%, PT Pesona Bangun Mandiri 4.43%, Public <5% 19.00%. Total 1,240,923,111 shares.
primary_business: Trading company - coffee beans (green beans) as 97% of 2024 revenue, with minor contributions from AirAlam bottled water (1.1%), Sunkist Water C vitamin drink (1%), and dried seaweed (0.4%). NOT a palm oil producer.
key_red_flags: NOT A PALM OIL COMPANY. WAPO is a coffee-bean (green bean) trading house with minor beverage/seaweed activity. No oil-palm plantations, CPO mills, FFB production or any palm processing. Revenue surged 404% YoY in 2024 driven by coffee-bean trade volumes (30,000 t), but margins remain razor-thin (gross margin 1.2%, operating margin 0.02%). Only 9 employees. Ticker should likely be EXCLUDED from the Indonesian palm long-list - clearly does not belong in palm oil peer set.
overall_comment: PT Wahana Pronatural Tbk posted 2024 net sales of Rp 2,122.33 bn (+404% YoY), almost entirely driven by green-bean coffee trading (30,000 t at inflated global prices; 97% of revenue). Gross profit Rp 24.65 bn, operating profit Rp 0.52 bn (vs Rp -0.36 bn loss in 2023), and net profit Rp 0.72 bn (comprehensive Rp 0.78 bn). Total assets fell to Rp 86.59 bn from Rp 131.94 bn as trade receivables collapsed from Rp 61.1 bn to Rp 4.7 bn. No palm-oil operations anywhere. Company has a negligible employee count (9) and trades commodities rather than producing them.

hq: Kisaran, North Sumatra (head office) / Jakarta corporate center (Bakrie Tower, Rasuna Epicentrum)
group_structure_note: Part of Bakrie Group (controlled by PT Bakrie Capital Indonesia 17.82%; ultimate controllers Bakrie family - Anindya N. Bakrie on BoC). Numerous subsidiaries in plantations, oleochemicals (until Jul 2024), and offshore finance SPVs (BSP Finance BV, Agri International Finance BV, Agri Resources BV in Netherlands; Bookwise/Fordway/Great Four in BVI/Mauritius).
subsidiaries_note: Plantation subs: PT Agrowiyana (AGW), PT Bakrie Pasaman Plantations (BPP), PT Air Muring (AM), PT Agro Mitra Madani (AMM), PT Grahadura Leidongprima, PT Eramitra Agrolestari, PT Huma Indah Mekar (rubber), PT Sumbertama Nusapertiwi, PT Jambi Agrowijaya, PT Monrad Intan Berakat, PT Citalaras Cipta Indonesia. Oleochemicals (deconsolidated Jul 2024): PT Bakrie Renewable Chemicals (BRC) - owns refinery/KCP, no longer a subsidiary after Jul 31 2024 following dilution. Finance SPVs: BSP Finance BV, BSP Netherlands Finance BV, BSP Liberia BV, Agri International Finance BV, Agri International Resources Pte Ltd (Singapore), Solegna Investments BV, Bookwise Investments Ltd, Fordway Management Ltd, Great Four International Investments Co Ltd. Others: PT Sarana Industama Perkasa, PT Sawitmas Agro Perkasa, PT Multrada Multi Maju, PT Padang Bolak Jaya, PT Perjapin Prima, PT Domas Sawitinti Perdana, PT Flora Sawita Chemindo, PT Inti Kemitraan Perdana, PT Trimitra Sumberperkasa, PT Bakrie Food and Energy (formerly PT Domas Agrointi Perkasa), PT ASD Bakrie Oil Palm Seed Indonesia (seed JV).
shareholder_structure_note: PT Bakrie Capital Indonesia 17.82% (445,562,965 shares), Ernawati Ali 10.96%, Stefanie Djajali 9.21%, Paradiso Resources Ltd. 6.12%, Public <5% 55.89%. Total 2,500,162,344 shares (Series A 1,372,047,138 + Series B 1,128,115,206). Classes: Local individuals 54.91%, Indonesian institutions 30.38%, Foreign institutions 13.84%, Foreign individuals 0.87%.
primary_business: Integrated plantation (oil palm + rubber); palm oil (CPO/PK) mills; oleochemical refinery (until Jul 31 2024 only - BRC deconsolidated)
key_red_flags: GOING CONCERN: Note 44 of audited 2024 FS contains going-concern qualification - Company has postponed payment of certain loan principals and/or interest; loans under restructuring (including Credit Suisse AG Singapore facility Rp3,371.95 bn and guaranteed equity-linked redeemable notes Rp1,284.22 bn). Net capital deficiency of Rp5,276.49 bn (improved from Rp5,932.87 bn in 2023). Current ratio only 10.44%. Liabilities-to-assets 264.17% (liabilities > assets). BRC deconsolidated Jul 31 2024 (lost control via dilution); wrote off Tebo (15,000 Ha) and Sarolangun (10,000 Ha) business-development projects due to revocation of plantation permits by Muara Tebo & Sarolangun regencies. USD 100 mn guaranteed equity-linked notes on SGX-ST since 2010 (8% p.a.) with no interest payments since 2014 (restructuring). Very large accumulated deficit (Rp12.66 trillion).
overall_comment: Highly distressed diversified Bakrie-group plantation (palm+rubber+oleochemicals). 2024 net sales Rp2,327.87 bn (-3.21%), gross profit Rp604.52 bn (+26.73% on cost efficiencies & ERP digitalization), net profit Rp138.89 bn (+431%). Continuing going concern uncertainty; capital deficiency Rp5,276 bn; liabilities Rp8,491 bn vs assets Rp3,214 bn. Oleochemical segment (BRC) deconsolidated after Jul 31 2024; palm now dominant segment (72.9% of sales). Planted area 56,147 Ha (palm 43,745 Ha inti + 14,975 Ha plasma = 58,720 Ha gross incl. plasma; rubber 12,403 Ha). Operated 5 palm mills (240 tph) and 1 rubber processing plant. FFB 347,865 t; CPO 113,918 t; PK 24,263 t. Closing price Rp97. No dividends paid since 2012.
business_model: Integrated (upstream plantations + downstream oleochemicals until mid-2024; post-Jul 2024 mainly upstream palm+rubber)

group_structure_note: Controlled by PT Sekawan Kontrindo (78.54%); individual shareholders Efendi 0.35% and Burhan 0.35%; Public 20.76%. No subsidiaries; one associate entity PT Sumber Enim Alam Lestari.
subsidiaries_note: No subsidiaries. Associate: PT Sumber Enim Alam Lestari (2015 est., palm plantation ~1,000 ha in Gumai/Glumbang, Muara Enim, South Sumatra).
shareholder_structure_note: PT Sekawan Kontrindo 78.54%, Efendi 0.35%, Burhan 0.35%, Public 20.76% (2,167,514,856 shares outstanding at end-2024).
primary_business: Oil palm plantation (upstream FFB producer); palm oil mill (10 tph) under construction, target operational Q1 2026.
key_red_flags: Very small scale (~1,090 ha planted, 882.58 ha HGU). FFB production fell 13.73% YoY to 22,461 t. Single-segment FFB seller to third-party mills (PT GAS, PT DSAP). No ISPO/RSPO certification. No industry association membership. No own mill yet (under construction, 70-83% progress). Board meeting frequency minimal (2 times/yr).
overall_comment: Tiny recent-IPO palm plantation (listed Oct 2023) in South Sumatra. 2024 revenue Rp63.58 bn (+3.1%), net profit Rp27.13 bn (+3.6%), total assets Rp190.56 bn. Currently a pure FFB seller; Rp46.6 bn of Rp85.1 bn IPO net proceeds deployed on 10 tph palm oil mill construction (70-83% complete at year-end). FFB volumes fell 13.7% but higher avg FFB selling price (+15.8%) lifted margins. Very low leverage (gearing 2.92%). Planted area only 1,090.64 ha.

group_structure_note: Founder-family controlled: Wilson Sutantio (22.76%), Hasan Tantri (19.48%), Charles Sutantio (17.61%), Peter Unggul Sutantio (15.27%); Public 24.88%. Listed on IDX since 31 August 2020 (IPO).
subsidiaries_note: PT Sriwijaya Nusantara Sejahtera (SNS) 99% - Palm Plantations; PT Hamparan Mutiara Hijau (HMH) 99% - Palm Plantations; PT Musi Andalan Sumatera (MAS) 99% - Palm Plantations. All domiciled in Palembang, Sumatra Selatan.
shareholder_structure_note: Wilson Sutantio 22.76%, Hasan Tantri 19.48%, Charles Sutantio 17.61%, Peter Unggul Sutantio 15.27%, Public 24.88%
primary_business: Palm oil plantation (upstream) + rubber plantation + crumb rubber / RSS + compost (ImproBio)
key_red_flags: FFB core production dropped 14% due to prolonged dry/El Nino climate; 53% of FFB processed sourced from third parties (limited self-sufficiency); revenue concentrated in two volatile commodities (palm + rubber); controlling family ownership ~75%.
overall_comment: South Sumatra-based integrated palm + rubber producer. 2024 revenue nearly flat at Rp2.04 tn; net profit +15% YoY to Rp221 bn driven by rubber margin recovery (RSS sales +120%) and fair-value gain on biological assets. Palm CPO production -4% to 95,507 t due to lower nucleus FFB (weather). Single PMKS mill (120 tph, 83.5 tph used), 1 crumb rubber factory (6,000 tpm), 1 RSS factory (600 tpm). DER 0.83.

group_structure_note: Subsidiary of PT Sawit Sumbermas Sarana Tbk (SSMS, IDX: SSMS) under PT Citra Borneo Indah (CBI) group. Raw materials sourced from SSMS plantations.
subsidiaries_note: None (standalone company financial statements)
shareholder_structure_note: PT Sawit Sumbermas Sarana Tbk 70.22%; PT Citra Borneo Indah 1.78%; PT Kalimantan Sawit Abadi 4.00%; PT Mitra Mendawai Sejati 4.00%; Maybank Global Growth Fund Singapore 11.01%; Public <5% 4.09% (new major shareholder MGGF emerged in 2025)
primary_business: Palm oil refinery, fractionation, kernel crushing and (new) packaged cooking oil ('Hanau' brand launched 2025)
key_red_flags: Production 603,670 MT still only 71% of 850,000 MT target; parent-level receivables to SSMS related party increased materially (Rp1,754,702m); rising short-term debt 2024; cooking oil venture is nascent; reliance on SSMS for CPO supply and sales channels.
overall_comment: Strong 2025 recovery: revenue +43% YoY to Rp13,971bn, operating profit +255% to Rp366bn, net profit +56% to Rp106bn. Refinery utilization recovered to 71% (from 48%), fractionation to 67% (from 43%), kernel crushing to 51%. Launched downstream packaged cooking oil 'Hanau' with new bottling plant (capacity 222,222 L/day). Maybank Global Growth Fund emerged as 11.01% shareholder in public float.

core_region: Kalimantan & Papua (mills); plantations in Kalimantan, Sumatra, Sulawesi, Papua

subsidiaries_note: BLP, BHL, ADS, SMS, MAJ, SGA, JMS, PLS, KAPAG, STP, SKS, TSP, PSR, VMA, MSP, SHM, SGSS, PAK, GEH, GES.
shareholder_structure_note: Issued shares 31,525,291,000; ultimate controller PT Rajawali Corpora (indirectly via Rajawali Capital International).
primary_business: Oil palm plantations, palm oil mills producing CPO, PK; wholesale of vegetable oils/fats.
key_red_flags: Accumulated deficit Rp 3,708 bn; gross debt ~Rp 4,035 bn (incl. Rp 200 bn Bonds I Phase I, Rp 50 bn Phase II, Rp 75 bn Sukuk Mudharabah I); interest expense Rp 425.3 bn absorbs ~36% of operating profit.
overall_comment: 2025 net sales Rp 5,761.3 bn (+33.9% YoY); net profit Rp 379.1 bn (+39.3%); EPS Rp 11.62 vs Rp 8.36. Two sukuk/bond offerings in 2025. Planted area note: 74,339 ha at 31-Dec-2025 and 2024 (NB: 2024 AR stated 87,000 ha; 2025 consolidated note specifies 74,339 ha - likely the precise net planted area vs rounded operational map).

shareholder_structure_note: PT Astra International Tbk ~79.68% (controlling), Public ~20.32%
primary_business: Palm oil plantation (upstream) + CPO/PKO refining + NPK blending
key_red_flags: Ageing plantation profile (avg age 16.7 yrs, ~71% of mature area >15 yrs); structural decline risk in productivity; dependence on replanting & 3rd-party FFB.
overall_comment: Strong 2025: revenue +31% to Rp28.7 tn, net profit +28% to Rp1.47 tn on +11% CPO ASP and +13% sales volume. Company repaid all bank loans; gearing <1%, current ratio 4.3x, cash Rp2.76 tn. 64% domestic / 36% export. FFB total 3.82 mt (+2%), third-party FFB 2.41 mt (+11%), CPO 1.20 mt.

core_region: Central Kalimantan (Kotawaringin Barat, Lamandau, Pulang Pisau)
group_structure_note: Part of Citra Borneo Indah (CBI) Group controlled by Abdul Rasyid family. PT Citra Borneo Indah 65.83% (parent). In 2025, SSMS completed acquisition of PT Sawit Mandiri Lestari (SML) as inorganic growth, adding a new estate with 6 estates + 2 mills in Lamandau. Downstream refinery operations run via PT Citra Borneo Utama Tbk (CBUT, consolidated Dec 2023).
subsidiaries_note: Existing subsidiaries: PT Mitra Mendawai Sejati (MMS), PT Kalimantan Sawit Abadi (KSA), PT Mirza Pratama Putra (MPP), PT Sawit Multi Utama (SMU), PT Tanjung Sawit Abadi (TSA), PT Menteng Kencana Mas (MKM), SSMS Plantation Holding Pte Ltd (SPH), SSMS Plantation International (SPI), Sawit Sumbermas International (SSI), PT Citra Borneo Utama Tbk (CBUT). NEW in 2025: PT Sawit Mandiri Lestari (SML) acquired for Rp1.49 tn cash — 6 estates + 2 mills in Lamandau.
shareholder_structure_note: PT Citra Borneo Indah 65.83%; PT Putra Borneo Agro Lestari 10.13%; Public (each <5%) 24.04%. CBI increased from 62.3% (2024) following share accumulation. 9,525,000,000 shares outstanding.
listed_status: Listed (IDX: SSMS). Trading suspended 4 Feb 2025 briefly on cumulative price surge.
primary_business: Integrated palm oil plantation + CPO mills + refinery/fractionation/kernel crushing (via CBUT) + biogas/Bio-CNG + new plantation expansion (SML acquisition)
key_red_flags: Major debt increase: gross debt rose to Rp8,139 bn (from Rp6,985 bn in 2024) via syndicated bank loan to fund SML acquisition. Equity dropped Rp344 bn at parent level due to common-control acquisition adjustment (Rp1,084 bn reduction in APIC). PKO production -24.57% due to KCP facility repairs/modifications. DER back up to 361.88% from 306.90%. 2024 share price was briefly suspended Feb 2025 due to 'significant cumulative price increase'.
overall_comment: Central Kalimantan palm producer controlled by CBI Group delivered strong 2025 turnaround: revenue Rp14.81 tn (+42.9%), operating profit Rp2.55 tn (+44%), net profit Rp1.39 tn (+67%) driven by CPO price recovery and volume growth post-CBUT consolidation full year benefit. OER improved to 22.12%. CPO production +14.23% to 527,052 t. Major SML acquisition for Rp1.49 tn expanded footprint to 136,447 ha total managed area (+18% YoY). Cash dividend resumed: Rp450 bn (Rp47.24/share). Syndicated bank loan supported expansion. 10 mills total (8 SSMS + 2 from SML).

group_structure_note: Part of Triputra Group (Theodore P. Rachmat family). Controlling shareholders (Dec 2025): PT Persada Capital Investama 33.07%, PT Triputra Investindo Arya 24.06%, PT Daya Adicipta Mustika 19.94%, T. Permadi Rachmat 5.09%; Public 17.84%.
subsidiaries_note: Direct/indirect subsidiaries including AMP (94.93% main holding) with downstream plantation subsidiaries (GBSM, MIK, TAN, FLTI, SKM, EBL, DLJ, NPN, MSL, KSD, HPM, SAWA, KAM, PTA, YWA, AAPA, GAS, BBB); HPS laboratory; ecosystem restoration (ATS, ABM, SAK, BSM); mining (BBP, PMB); trading (APR); TAPAS IT. USTP 50% JV, ATP Bio Indonesia 49% JV for biocoke production.
shareholder_structure_note: PT Persada Capital Investama 33.07%, PT Triputra Investindo Arya 24.06%, PT Daya Adicipta Mustika 19.94%, T. Permadi Rachmat 5.09%, Public 17.84%
primary_business: Palm oil plantations (upstream) + CPO/PK/PKO processing + rubber + renewable energy (biocoke via JV)
key_red_flags: Nucleus planted area declined slightly to 132.8k ha (from 135.6k in 2024) reflecting replanting; long-term bank loans up 132% to Rp766 bn (from Rp330 bn); aging tree profile still concentrated at 7-20 yr (115.8k ha); ESG-related challenges from global policy tightening (B50 mandate, EU deforestation regulation).
overall_comment: Mid-large Indonesian palm producer controlled by Triputra Group (Rachmat family). 2025 revenue Rp11.40 tn (+18% YoY), net profit Rp3.85 tn (+19%) driven by higher CPO (+8%), PK (+52%) and PKO (+37%) selling prices and volume growth. Total planted 159.7k ha (132.8k nucleus + 26.9k plasma). 18 palm oil mills with 995 tph. CPO 953k tons (+4%), PK 202k tons (+5%), PKO 22k tons (+10%); FFB yield improved to 24.3 t/ha (from 23.0). Very low gearing - net debt/equity -0.047x (net cash). 2026 strategy: build new mill in East Kalimantan, downstreaming feasibility study, replanting with superior varieties. Strong share price rally in 2025 (Q3 high Rp1,715, closed Rp1,500). 2025 interim dividends Rp89 total (Rp50+Rp39).

hq: Jakarta, Indonesia (Beltway Office Park Tower B, Jl. TB Simatupang No. 41, Jakarta Selatan 12550)
core_region: East Kalimantan (Berau, Kutai Timur, Paser, Kutai Kartanegara)
group_structure_note: Holding company with 10 subsidiaries (new: PT Cipta Davia Mandiri acquired Jan 2025); controlled by PT Teladan Resources (59.877%) which is owned and controlled by Wiwoho Basuki Tjokronegoro and family. Listed on IDX since 12 April 2022.

shareholder_structure_note: PT Teladan Resources 59.877%, Ahmad Gunung 16.225%, Wishnu Wardhana 16.225%, Wiwoho Basuki Tjokronegoro 0.169%, Public 7.504%
primary_business: Oil palm plantation, palm oil processing (CPO, PK, CPKO, PKE), renewable energy (biomass/biogas)
key_red_flags: CPO extraction rate dipped to 22.76% (from 23.51%); PK extraction 3.57% (from 3.68%) — productivity slightly lower; ageing tree profile (nucleus avg age 16.1 yrs); revenue surge partly buoyed by one-off gain on bargain purchase (Rp172 bn) from CDM acquisition; CFF outflow high (Rp1.18 tn) from larger dividend payouts.
overall_comment: Breakout year: revenue Rp5.42 tn (+28.7%), driven by CPO price +14.5% to Rp14 mn/t, PK price +65.8%, and full-year KCP operation (CPKO 11,991 t; PKE 15,803 t). Net profit Rp1.11 tn (+34%), aided by Rp172 bn bargain purchase gain from CDM acquisition. Planted area expanded to 64,430 ha (+3,933 ha) via CDM acquisition. Second KCP and BPP kick-off Dec 2025. Equity reached Rp3.64 tn; bank debt/equity 0.23x. Declared first-ever interim dividend (Rp15.5/share, Rp201 bn).
